zh.Albury, Hertfordshire, England: 英國英格蘭賀特福爾阿爾布瑞
zh.Chorlton cum Hardy, Lancashire, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾霍爾頓 姆 哈代
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Sacheverell於大約1334年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾時，她的父親John Sacheverell27歲，母親Mrs. John Sacheverell23歲。她和Richard Piperell Barley至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於從1394年1月9日到1395年1月8日在英國英格蘭賀特福爾阿爾布瑞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Sacheverell于大约1334年出生在英国英格兰德比夏爾时，她的父亲John Sacheverell27岁，母亲Mrs. John Sacheverell23岁。她和Richard Piperell Barley至少有4个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于从1394年1月9日到1395年1月8日在英国英格兰賀特福爾阿爾布瑞。

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Sacheverell was born about 1334, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Sacheverell, was 27 and her mother, Mrs. John Sacheverell, was 23. She had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Richard Piperell Barley. She died from 9 January 1394 to 8 January 1395, in Albury, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Richard Piperell Barley. Elle est décédée du 9 janvier 1394 au 8 janvier 1395, à Albury, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hatte mit Richard Piperell Barley mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb von 9. Januar 1394 bis 8. Januar 1395 in Albury, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Sacheverell(이)가 대략 1334년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Sacheverell(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. John Sacheverell(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Richard Piperell Barley(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1394년 1월 9일부터 1395년 1월 8일까지 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 알버이에서 사망했다.
